CheckTitle: Review third-party service principals with application permissions
Description: Identifies non-first-party, non-managed-identity service principals that have been granted application-level permissions via appRoleAssignments. Third-party apps with app permissions operate without user context and should be regularly reviewed.
Risk: Third-party service principals with application permissions can access tenant data without user interaction. If a third-party vendor is compromised, granted application permissions provide a direct path to exfiltrate data or escalate privilege within the tenant.

Remediation.Recommendation: Review all third-party service principals with application permissions. Remove grants for apps that are no longer in use. Apply least-privilege by replacing broad permissions with narrower scopes. Consider using application access policies to restrict mailbox or site access.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark

SourceNotes: Framework control IDs were AI-generated. Third-party app permission review is a well-known Entra ID security practice.
